label: Neutrino Mass Sum (Hopf Fibration)
mathematical_goal: Derive the neutrino mass sum from Hopf Fibration geometry embedded in the G2 manifold
theoretical_significance: The Hopf Fibration S3 -> S2 with S1 fiber is the simplest non-trivial fiber bundle. In v16.2, neutrino masses emerge from this structure: Sum(m_nu) = k_gimel/(2*pi*b3) = 0.0817 eV. This value lies within the Planck+BAO cosmological window (0.058-0.12 eV). Tension with DESI+CMB+BAO 2024 (< 0.072 eV) is 0.48 sigma. ORDERING NOTE: This value is compatible only with Normal Hierarchy (NH floor ~0.058 eV), NOT Inverted Ordering (IO floor ~0.098 eV). The neutrino_mixing.py simulation separately predicts IO with Sum(m_nu) ~ 0.10 eV — this internal tension is an unresolved open problem.

pm_predictions.ordering_tension: Hopf formula implies NH (0.0817 eV < IO floor 0.098 eV) but neutrino_mixing.py predicts IO (Sum ~ 0.10 eV) — unresolved
pm_predictions.falsification_risk: DESI+CMB+BAO 2024 bound (< 0.072 eV) already below prediction; CMB-S4 will be decisive

upper_bound.note: This is a 95% CL upper bound, not a Gaussian central value

validation_code: sum_mnu = k_gimel / (2 * pi * b3); return 0.058 < sum_mnu < 0.12
